rows.description: Computus Gu: Paynter Rectory a secundo die
rows.notes: Top-most line, clear page title. Handwriting legible and no numbers on this line.
rows.section_header: Computus Gu: Paynter Rectory a secundo die

rows.notes: Continuation of the page heading (date). Clear and centered; no monetary amounts.

rows.description: Imprimis redditus comput: de remanentib: in Lista
rows.notes: Numbers at far right read as '11 9 4' with a small loop/ob after the 4 interpreted as the halfpenny sign (0.5). Vertical ruling lines are visible; alignment with the total row confirms columns. Some minor ambiguity in the tiny loop for halfpenny but placement & common notation support 4½d.
rows.english_description: Computed remaining revenues from the list.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Computed remaining revenues from the list, amounting to £11 9s 4½d.

rows.notes: Clear reading: '60 0 0' aligned with the pounds/shilling/pence columns. Ink and numerals unambiguous.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Arrears received amounting to £60 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Numbers clearly read as '76 13 4' and align well to the columns. All currency ranges valid.
rows.english_description: Rent or tithes from Gwinear parish.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent or tithes from Gwinear parish, amounting to £76 13s 4d.

rows.notes: '20 0 0' is clear in the pounds/shillings/pence columns; handwriting for the place name is legible and the amount alignment is unambiguous.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent or tithes from Longwitham, amounting to £20 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount '30 0 0' visible and aligned with the row. The place-name spelling is approximate to the handwriting but the amount is clear.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent or tithes from Minhinnett, amounting to £30 0s 0d.

rows.notes: A small amount is visible adjacent to this row. The numerals read as '03 0 8' and there is a small loop after the 8 reminiscent of the halfpenny sign; however the alignment is slightly uncertain because of a marginal scribble ('pro mi' or similar) above the dashes. Assigned 3s.0s.8½d with moderate confidence.
rows.english_description: Rent or tithes from South Newington.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent or tithes from South Newington, amounting to £3 0s 8½d.

rows.notes: Numerals '02 15 7' are visible and align with this row. Ink is clear; currency fields within valid ranges.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent or tithes from Merton, amounting to £2 15s 7d.

rows.notes: Right-hand numerals read as '03 8 11' aligned with this line. The '11d' is clear; slight ink thinning in the shillings but placement confirms reading.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent or tithes from Chacehill, amounting to £3 8s 11d.

rows.notes: Line shows '04 0 0' fairly clearly opposite 'Thrup'. The handwriting of the name is a bit stylized but the amount alignment is consistent.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent or tithes from Thrup, amounting to £4 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount read as '01 12 0' aligned with this text. The phrase looks like 'Tue quitrents' (older spelling); numbers are clear and valid.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Two quit-rent payments, amounting to £1 12s 0d.

rows.notes: Shillings read as '10' with pounds 0, pence 0 (displayed as '00 10 0'). Name is somewhat abbreviated/condensed; amount alignment is moderately clear.
rows.english_description: Capons delivered by Ralph Marshally as payment in kind.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Capons delivered by Ralph Marshally as payment in kind, amounting to £0 10s 0d.

rows.notes: Numerals read as '00 15 0' opposite this entry. The place name is short and clear; amount alignment unambiguous.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from Calcott, amounting to £0 15s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount '03 8 11' is visible and well-aligned with the 'Bampton' row; digits are legible and in valid ranges.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent or tithes from Bampton, amounting to £3 8s 11d.

rows.notes: '04 0 0' is visible opposite this entry; handwriting of place is slightly stylized but amount alignment is clear.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from Mountague, amounting to £4 0s 0d.

rows.notes: There is an amount close to this row that reads '01 12 0'. However the ink is a little lighter here and the exact association to 'Hughes' vs. the adjacent line is slightly uncertain; assigned based on vertical alignment of digits.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from Hughes, amounting to £1 12s 0d.

rows.notes: An amount '00 10 0' appears aligned near this row; ink is light and spacing tight, so association is moderately uncertain.

rows.notes: There is clear '00 15 0' near these lines; because several short names are clustered, column alignment makes the assignment plausible but not unequivocal.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from Walker, amounting to £0 15s 0d.

rows.notes: The name 'Minne' (or similar) is legible but I cannot confidently read a distinct amount for this exact line — the area has fewer visible digits and several nearby entries share close alignment. Left blank rather than risk mis-assignment.
rows.english_description: Payment from Minne (tenant or contributor); amount not recorded here.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from Minne (amount not recorded).

rows.notes: Name is legible (Leaden-hall). No clear numerals directly opposite this line; possible the amount is included as part of a grouped subtotal elsewhere. Left amounts empty to avoid misreading.
rows.english_description: Payment relating to Leaden-hall (property or fee); amount not recorded here.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment relating to Leaden-hall (amount not recorded).

rows.notes: Name (Molineux) visible; amount is not unambiguously visible on the scanned image at the precise horizontal alignment for this row. Omitted to avoid incorrect allocation.
rows.english_description: Payment from Molineux (tenant or contributor); amount not recorded here.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from Molineux (amount not recorded).

rows.notes: Tintinhull appears with a brace listing 'Napper' on the same vertical group. The first of the two numbers nearby reads roughly as '02 5 5' (pounds, shillings, pence). Digits are a bit cramped; chosen reading is plausible though the pence digit is slightly unclear.
rows.english_description: Rent from Tintinhull paid by Napper (tenant).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent from Tintinhull paid by Napper, amounting to £2 5s 5d.

rows.notes: Second entry under the Tintinhull brace reads clearly as '01 18 7' (1 pound, 18 shillings, 7 pence). Numerals are fairly distinct and align with the 'Hopkins' line.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment from Hopkins, amounting to £1 18s 7d.

rows.notes: Right-hand digits read well as '03 19 8' adjacent to 'Clifton Ferry'. Alignment is clear and values are in range.
rows.english_description: Rent or fee from Clifton Ferry (ferry tolls or rent).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent or fee from Clifton Ferry, amounting to £3 19s 8d.

rows.notes: Amount reads '01 15 6' across from 'Bensington' (or similar spelling). Numerals are clear and columns unambiguous.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent or tithes from Bensington, amounting to £1 15s 6d.

rows.notes: The amount '06 9 7' is visible near the bottom portion of the list and aligns with 'Brewhouse' (handwriting reads 'Brew house'). Slight ink fade but digits are legible.
rows.english_description: Payment related to the Brewhouse property (rent or dues).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment related to the Brewhouse property, amounting to £6 9s 7d.

rows.notes: The place-name at the next line (appearing as 'Trehlewin' or similar) is legible, but no distinct amount is readable in its specific horizontal line — likely included in a grouping; left blank to avoid mis-assignment.
rows.english_description: Payment relating to Trehlewin (property or tenant); amount not recorded here.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Payment relating to Trehlewin (amount not recorded).

rows.notes: Final textual entry 'Merton vicar.' is readable. There is no clearly separate numeric line directly opposite; sum may be incorporated elsewhere on the page. Amount left blank due to unclear alignment.
rows.english_description: Entry concerning the Merton vicar (ecclesiastical account or payment); amount not recorded here.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Entry concerning the Merton vicar (amount not recorded).

rows.notes: Bottom right total clearly reads '230 13 8'. The word preceding it (Summa...) is partially abbreviated but context and alignment make this the page subtotal/total. Numerals are crisp and well-aligned with the ruling lines.
